Track head SHA to detect cloud agent work completion on merge conflicts (#600)

* Initial plan

* Track head SHA in PendingMergeIssue to detect agent work completion

When the cloud_agent backend posts a @copilot comment on a conflicting
PR, we now record the PR's head SHA at comment time. In
promote_pending_merge_issues, if the PR still has conflicts but the head
SHA changed (indicating the agent pushed a change), the deduplication
guard is removed so a fresh comment can be posted. This prevents the
system from staying silent indefinitely when the agent finishes work but
fails to resolve conflicts.

* feat: selective Kustomization reconcile in workload watch

Map changed file paths to their nearest containing Kustomization
directory and scope kubectl apply to that subtree. Falls back to
full reconcile when the change is at the root level or no
kustomization.yaml boundary is found.

* fix: normalize paths and handle directory events in findKustomizationDir

- Use filepath.Abs to normalize changedFile and rootDir before comparison
- Handle directory-path events by starting search at the directory itself
- Update runKubectlApply doc comment to reflect subtree support
- Add test for directory-path event handling

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add deleted file fallback test for findKustomizationDir

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve all golangci-lint issues in workload watch

- Fix noinlineerr: use plain assignment instead of inline error handling
  in findKustomizationDir for os.Stat calls
- Fix funlen: split TestFindKustomizationDir (139 lines) into 8
  individual test functions to stay under the 60-line limit

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: avoid potential nil dereference in findKustomizationDir

Invert the condition to check statErr == nil && info.IsDir() instead of
statErr != nil || !info.IsDir() to prevent any possibility of nil
pointer access on info.

  successful role update, causing the user's next request to get a 401 which

- EditRole now finds all users assigned the edited role and invalidates their

* fix: correctly extract LoginResponseDTO from API response wrapper in refresh token interceptor

The v1UsersRefreshTokenList() endpoint returns LoginResponseDTOResponse
(shape: { success, data: LoginResponseDTO, message, errors }).
The interceptor was casting response.data as LoginResponseDTO, so user/
accessToken/refreshToken all resolved to undefined → null, clearing
localStorage on every successful token refresh.

Fix: access response.data.data (the inner LoginResponseDTO) so the correct
fields are read and localStorage is updated instead of cleared.

fix: version-aware push prevents host from overwriting remote changes (#57)

* fix: version-aware push prevents host from overwriting remote changes

Replace fire-and-forget syncGame push with a version-aware push that
checks the server version before pushing. When the debounced push fires:

1. Fetch current server version via pullGameVersion
2. If server version > lastServerVersion, pull full server state and
   apply it locally (SYNC_GAME) instead of pushing stale data
3. If server is not ahead, push with X-Expected-Version header
4. Handle 409 conflict by pulling server state

Also adds optional expectedVersion parameter to pushGame() so the
caller can specify the version independently of game.version.

Fixes conditional hook calls in useApiSync (rules-of-hooks) by moving
sync-disabled noop functions to module level.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: pass inPlayCharacterIds to CharacterAssignmentDialog (#56)

* fix: pass inPlayCharacterIds to CharacterAssignmentDialog

The CharacterAssignmentDialog has built-in logic to filter characters
by inPlayCharacterIds (including duplicate support for Legion etc.),
but GameViewPage was not passing that prop. Instead it pre-filtered
via an assignmentCharacterDefs memo, but the dialog's internal
availableCharacters computation fell through to the else branch,
showing all script characters instead of only the selected ones.

Fix: pass scriptCharacterDefs (full script) + inPlayCharacterIds to
let the dialog's own filtering handle it correctly. Remove the now
unnecessary assignmentCharacterDefs memo.

commit.message:
[build] E: Batch cargo invocations

Replace per-package cargo check/build/clippy/fmt invocations with
batched multi-package calls using repeated -p flags. Packages sharing
the same target triple and feature flags are grouped into a single
cargo invocation. This eliminates redundant workspace resolution,
dependency fingerprinting, and lock acquisition overhead.

Before: 62 separate cargo invocations for `make check`
After:  16 batched cargo invocations for `make check`

The batched -p flag lists are auto-generated from the same top-level
package lists (ALL_GUEST_RUST_LIBS, ALL_HOST_BINARIES, etc.) that
drove the previous foreach macros. Adding a new package still only
requires appending to the appropriate list.

Individual per-package targets (e.g., check-guest-rlib-nvx) are
preserved for ad-hoc developer use.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
